⚔️ Service Comparison

🆚 AWS CodeBuild vs Jenkins
Feature AWS CodeBuild Jenkins Winner
Setup Time 10 minutes (managed) 2-4 hours (self-managed) ✅ CodeBuild
Infrastructure Fully managed, serverless Self-managed servers ✅ CodeBuild
Cost (anihpj) $0.60 - $2.50/month $100+ /month (EC2 + maintenance) ✅ CodeBuild (98% cheaper)
Scaling Automatic, infinite Manual, limited by instances ✅ CodeBuild
Maintenance Zero (AWS managed) 8-10 hrs/month (patches, plugins) ✅ CodeBuild
Security Updates Automatic Manual ✅ CodeBuild
AWS Integration Native (IAM, S3, ECR, etc.) Requires plugins & config ✅ CodeBuild
Plugin Ecosystem Limited Extensive (1800+ plugins) ✅ Jenkins
Custom Workflows Good (buildspec) Excellent (Groovy pipelines) ✅ Jenkins
UI/UX Simple (AWS Console) Feature-rich (Blue Ocean) ✅ Jenkins
Build Speed Fast (ARM Graviton2) Depends on instance ⚖️ Tie
Docker Support Native, privileged mode Requires Docker plugin ✅ CodeBuild
🆚 AWS CodeBuild vs GitHub Actions
Feature AWS CodeBuild GitHub Actions Winner
Free Tier 100 build minutes/month 2,000 minutes/month (public), 3,000 (private) ✅ GitHub Actions
Paid Pricing $0.005/min (arm1.small) $0.008/min (Linux) ✅ CodeBuild (60% cheaper)
AWS Integration Native, seamless Requires OIDC setup ✅ CodeBuild
Marketplace Limited actions 10,000+ actions ✅ GitHub Actions
Workflow Syntax buildspec.yml YAML workflows ⚖️ Tie (both YAML)
Matrix Builds Manual setup Native matrix support ✅ GitHub Actions
Caching S3/local cache Built-in actions/cache ✅ GitHub Actions (easier)
Build Artifacts S3 storage GitHub artifacts ⚖️ Tie
Private VPC Native support Requires self-hosted runners ✅ CodeBuild
ARM Support ARM Graviton2 (70% cheaper) Limited ARM support ✅ CodeBuild
Secrets Management Parameter Store, Secrets Manager GitHub Secrets ⚖️ Tie
Community Smaller Large, active ✅ GitHub Actions
🆚 AWS CodeBuild vs GitLab CI
Feature AWS CodeBuild GitLab CI Winner
Hosting AWS only GitLab.com or self-hosted ✅ GitLab (flexibility)
Free Tier 100 minutes/month 400 minutes/month ✅ GitLab
AWS Integration Native Manual setup ✅ CodeBuild
Pipeline Syntax buildspec.yml .gitlab-ci.yml ⚖️ Tie
Docker Registry ECR integration Built-in GitLab Registry ⚖️ Tie
Cost (Medium Project) ~$5/month ~$19/month (Premium) ✅ CodeBuild
DevOps Features Build only Full DevOps platform ✅ GitLab
Auto DevOps Manual setup One-click Auto DevOps ✅ GitLab
🎯 Decision Guide: Which Build Service?
🔍 Choose AWS CodeBuild if:
  • ✅ Your infrastructure is primarily on AWS
  • ✅ You want zero maintenance overhead
  • ✅ Cost optimization is critical ($0.005/min for ARM)
  • ✅ You need seamless AWS service integration (S3, ECR, Lambda, etc.)
  • ✅ You prefer serverless, auto-scaling builds
  • ✅ You need VPC isolation for private builds
  • ✅ You're building Docker images frequently
  • ✅ ARM-based builds are important (70% cost savings)
🔧 Choose Jenkins if:
  • ✅ You need extensive plugin ecosystem (1800+ plugins)
  • ✅ You have complex, custom workflow requirements
  • ✅ You prefer self-hosted, full control
  • ✅ You're using multiple cloud providers
  • ✅ You have dedicated DevOps team for maintenance
  • ✅ You need advanced pipeline visualization (Blue Ocean)
🐙 Choose GitHub Actions if:
  • ✅ Your code is hosted on GitHub
  • ✅ You want generous free tier (2000-3000 min/month)
  • ✅ You need matrix builds across multiple versions
  • ✅ You prefer marketplace of 10,000+ actions
  • ✅ You want tight GitHub integration (PR comments, status checks)
  • ✅ Your team is already familiar with GitHub workflows
🦊 Choose GitLab CI if:
  • ✅ You want full DevOps platform (Git + CI/CD + Registry + Security)
  • ✅ You prefer self-hosted control
  • ✅ You need Auto DevOps (zero-config pipelines)
  • ✅ You want built-in container registry
  • ✅ You're using GitLab for source control
🎯 For anihpj Django Job Post Platform

Recommendation: AWS CodeBuild

Why?

  • ✅ Cost: $0.60-$2.50/month vs $100+/month for Jenkins (98% savings)
  • ✅ Zero Maintenance: No servers to manage, patch, or monitor
  • ✅ AWS Ecosystem: Seamless integration with EC2, RDS, S3, ECR
  • ✅ ARM Graviton2: 70% cost savings with arm1.small instances
  • ✅ Fast Builds: 2-3 minutes with caching vs 8-10 minutes before
  • ✅ Scalability: Automatic scaling for concurrent builds
  • ✅ Security: Native IAM, VPC, KMS integration
  • ✅ Simple Setup: 10 minutes vs hours for Jenkins

Result: 10-15 deployments/day with 99.5% success rate and $117.50/month saved! 🎉

🚀 AWS CodeDeploy - Automated Deployment Service

Deployment CI/CD Fully Managed Auto-Rollback Multi-Platform
FREE
For EC2/On-Premises
$0.02
Per Lambda/ECS Deployment
100%
Managed Service
Multi-Region
Global Deployment
Zero-Downtime
Rolling Deployments
2-10 min
Typical Deploy Time
🏆

Automated Deployments with Zero-Downtime Updates

AWS CodeDeploy automates deployments to EC2, Lambda, ECS, and on-premises servers. Deploy with confidence using rolling updates, blue/green deployments, and automatic rollback on failure. Perfect for anihpj Django's production deployment workflow with zero user impact.

No Downtime
Auto-Rollback
Multi-Platform
Category: Developer Tools - Deployment Use Case: Automated application deployments to EC2, Lambda, ECS, and on-premises Pricing: Free for EC2/on-premises, $0.02/deployment for Lambda/ECS Last Updated: December 2025 📚 Official Documentation

For anihpj: Automate Django application deployments to EC2 instances with zero-downtime rolling updates, automatic rollback on failure, and deployment tracking. Perfect for coordinating database migrations and application updates.

📋 Overview & Introduction

AWS CodeDeploy is a fully managed deployment service that automates software deployments to various compute services including Amazon EC2, AWS Fargate, AWS Lambda, and on-premises servers. CodeDeploy eliminates the need for error-prone manual operations and scales to match your deployment needs.

🚀 Quick Start (10 Minutes):
  1. Install CodeDeploy agent on EC2 instances
  2. Create IAM roles (CodeDeploy service, EC2 instance)
  3. Create CodeDeploy application and deployment group
  4. Create appspec.yml in project root
  5. Push code to S3 or GitHub → Trigger deployment
🎯 CodeDeploy at a Glance
🤖 Automation
Zero-touch deploys
One-click rollback
Automated scaling
No manual SSH
🎯 Deployment Strategies
In-place updates
Blue/Green deploys
Rolling updates
Canary deployments
💰 Cost Effective
Free for EC2
$0.02 per Lambda
No infrastructure
Pay per deploy
🔄 Reliability
Auto rollback
Health checks
Stop on failure
Traffic shifting
🔗 Integration
CodePipeline native
GitHub, S3 source
ELB integration
CloudWatch logs
🌍 Multi-Platform
EC2 instances
Lambda functions
ECS containers
On-premises servers
💡 Why Choose CodeDeploy for anihpj?
  • Zero-Downtime Deployments: Rolling updates keep job portal available during Django updates
  • Automated Rollback: Automatically revert to previous version if deployment fails
  • Database Migration Safety: Run Django migrations before serving traffic with lifecycle hooks
  • Cost Savings: Free for EC2 deployments (no additional infrastructure costs)
  • Traffic Control: Gradually shift traffic to new version (10% → 50% → 100%)
  • Integration Ready: Works seamlessly with CodePipeline, GitHub, and S3
📊 Key Benefits & Decision Tree
START: Need to deploy application updates?
│
├─ Currently deploying manually?
│  ├─ YES → ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY (Critical Priority)
│  │        Problem: SSH to each server, manual commands, downtime
│  │        Solution: Automated rolling deploys to 10+ servers
│  │        Time Saved: 2 hours manual → 10 min automated
│  │        Cost: FREE for EC2 deployments
│  │
│  └─ NO → Already have deployment automation?
│     ├─ Using custom scripts (bash/Ansible)?
│     │  └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY (Upgrade Recommended)
│     │     Benefits: Built-in rollback, health checks, ELB integration
│     │     Migration: Convert scripts to appspec.yml hooks
│     │
│     └─ Using Jenkins/GitLab CI?
│        └─ CONSIDER CODEDEPLOY if:
│           ├─ Need blue/green deployments (zero downtime)
│           ├─ Want AWS-native integration (CodePipeline)
│           └─ Need advanced traffic shifting (canary/linear)
│
├─ Deployment frequency?
│  ├─ Multiple times per day?
│  │  └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY (Essential)
│  │     Setup: In-place rolling updates
│  │     Result: Deploy in 5-10 minutes with zero downtime
│  │     Safety: Auto-rollback on any failure
│  │
│  ├─ Weekly/monthly releases?
│  │  └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY (Highly Recommended)
│  │     Setup: Blue/green with manual approval
│  │     Result: Test new version, switch traffic when ready
│  │     Rollback: Instant (just switch back to blue)
│  │
│  └─ Rare deployments (quarterly)?
│     └─ OPTIONAL - Manual may work
│        But: CodeDeploy adds safety even for rare deploys
│
├─ Infrastructure size?
│  ├─ 1-2 EC2 instances?
│  │  └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY (Free, worth it)
│  │     Setup time: 30 minutes
│  │     Deploy time: 3 minutes (vs. 15 min manual)
│  │     Cost: $0 (free for EC2)
│  │
│  ├─ 3-10 instances?
│  │  └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY (Mandatory)
│  │     Prevents: SSH to 10 servers manually (30+ min)
│  │     Enables: Rolling updates (one at a time)
│  │     Ensures: Always have servers running (HA)
│  │
│  └─ 10+ instances (or Auto Scaling)?
│     └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY (Critical)
│        Scenario: Auto Scaling launches new instances
│        CodeDeploy: Automatically deploys latest version
│        Without: New instances have old code (disaster!)
│
├─ Deployment type?
│  ├─ EC2-based web application (Django)?
│  │  └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY
│  │     Strategy: In-place rolling (one instance at a time)
│  │     Hooks: BeforeInstall → Install → AfterInstall → ApplicationStart
│  │     Use case: anihpj Django app behind ALB
│  │
│  ├─ Lambda functions?
│  │  └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY
│  │     Strategy: Linear (shift 10% traffic every 10 min)
│  │     Rollback: Automatic if CloudWatch alarms trigger
│  │     Cost: $0.02 per deployment
│  │
│  ├─ ECS containers?
│  │  └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY
│  │     Strategy: Blue/green with ECS
│  │     Test environment: Create new task set
│  │     Switch traffic: Update ALB target groups
│  │
│  └─ On-premises servers?
│     └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY
│        Agent: Install on your servers
│        Benefit: Same deployment process (cloud + on-prem)
│
└─ Deployment safety requirements?
   ├─ Need automatic rollback?
   │  └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY
   │     Monitors: CloudWatch alarms, health checks
   │     Triggers: Rollback if CPU spikes, errors increase
   │     Result: Bad deployment auto-reverts in 2 minutes
   │
   ├─ Need canary testing?
   │  └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY
   │     Pattern: Deploy to 10% of fleet first
   │     Wait: 5 minutes, monitor errors
   │     Proceed: If healthy, deploy to remaining 90%
   │
   └─ Need blue/green (instant rollback)?
      └─ ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY
         Blue: Current version (running)
         Green: New version (deploy + test)
         Switch: All traffic to green (instant)
         Rollback: Switch back to blue (30 seconds)

🎯 RECOMMENDATION FOR ANIHPJ:
└─ Django job portal with:
   ├─ 3 EC2 instances behind Application Load Balancer
   ├─ PostgreSQL database (RDS)
   ├─ Static assets (S3 + CloudFront)
   └─ Deployed 2-3 times per week
   
   ✅ USE CODEDEPLOY (Mandatory for Production)
   
   Deployment strategy:
   ├─ Type: In-place rolling update
   ├─ Policy: OneAtATime (deploy to 1 instance at a time)
   ├─ ELB: Remove instance → Deploy → Add back
   ├─ Hooks:
   │  ├─ ApplicationStop: Stop Gunicorn
   │  ├─ BeforeInstall: Backup database
   │  ├─ AfterInstall: pip install, collectstatic
   │  ├─ ApplicationStart: Migrate DB, start Gunicorn
   │  └─ ValidateService: Health check /api/health/
   │
   Benefits:
   ├─ Zero downtime: ALB always has 2 healthy instances
   ├─ Safe migrations: Run on first instance, validate
   ├─ Auto rollback: If /api/health/ fails
   ├─ Deploy time: 15 min (vs. 45 min manual)
   ├─ Cost: $0 (free for EC2)
   └─ Audit trail: CloudWatch logs of every deployment
🏗️ CodeDeploy Architecture for anihpj Django
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    AWS CODEDEPLOY - ANIHPJ DEPLOYMENT ARCHITECTURE                       │
│                     Zero-Downtime Django Application Deployment                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                          │
│  📦 DEPLOYMENT SOURCE (Trigger)                                                          │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  1️⃣ GitHub Repository                2️⃣ S3 Bucket                                  │ │
│  │     └─ anihpj/jobpost                   └─ s3://anihpj-deployments/               │ │
│  │        ├─ appspec.yml                      ├─ anihpj-v2.3.zip                     │ │
│  │        ├─ scripts/                         └─ appspec.yml                         │ │
│  │        │  ├─ stop_server.sh                                                       │ │
│  │        │  ├─ install_dependencies.sh    3️⃣ CodePipeline                            │ │
│  │        │  ├─ start_server.sh               └─ Source → Build → Deploy             │ │
│  │        │  └─ validate_service.sh                                                  │ │
│  │        ├─ requirements.txt                                                        │ │
│  │        └─ anihpj/ (Django project)                                                │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  Trigger Methods:                                                                 │ │
│  │  • GitHub webhook → CodeDeploy                                                    │ │
│  │  • S3 upload → EventBridge → CodeDeploy                                           │ │
│  │  • CodePipeline deploy stage                                                      │ │
│  │  • Manual trigger (AWS Console/CLI)                                               │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                         │                                               │
│                                         ▼                                               │
│  🎯 CODEDEPLOY APPLICATION & DEPLOYMENT GROUP                                           │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Application: anihpj-jobportal                                                     │ │
│  │  Deployment Group: anihpj-prod-dg                                                  │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  Configuration:                                                                    │ │
│  │  ├─ Deployment Type: In-place (rolling update)                                    │ │
│  │  ├─ Deployment Config: CodeDeployDefault.OneAtATime                               │ │
│  │  ├─ Environment: EC2 instances with tag "App=anihpj"                              │ │
│  │  ├─ Load Balancer: Application Load Balancer (anihpj-alb)                         │ │
│  │  ├─ Auto Rollback: Enabled (on deployment failure or alarm)                       │ │
│  │  └─ CloudWatch Alarms: CPU > 80%, 5xx errors > 10                                 │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                         │                                               │
│                                         ▼                                               │
│  🔄 DEPLOYMENT EXECUTION (Rolling Update - OneAtATime)                                  │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │  │  TARGET ENVIRONMENT: 3 EC2 Instances behind ALB                              │ │ │
│  │  │                                                                              │ │ │
│  │  │  ┌─────────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  EC2 Instance 1     │  │  EC2 Instance 2     │  │  EC2 Instance 3     │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  us-east-1a         │  │  us-east-1b         │  │  us-east-1c         │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │                     │  │                     │  │                     │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  ✅ Version: 2.2     │  │  ✅ Version: 2.2     │  │  ✅ Version: 2.2     │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  Status: Healthy    │  │  Status: Healthy    │  │  Status: Healthy    │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  CodeDeploy Agent   │  │  CodeDeploy Agent   │  │  CodeDeploy Agent   │ │ │ │
│  │  │  └─────────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘ │ │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │  │  🔵 PHASE 1: Deploy to Instance 1 (us-east-1a)                               │ │ │
│  │  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  Step 1: ApplicationStop (30 sec)                                      │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  └─ Run: scripts/stop_server.sh                                        │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ sudo systemctl stop gunicorn                                    │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ sudo systemctl stop nginx                                       │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     ✅ Gunicorn stopped, Nginx stopped                                  │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │                                                                        │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  Step 2: DownloadBundle (20 sec)                                       │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  └─ Download from: s3://anihpj-deployments/anihpj-v2.3.zip            │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  └─ Extract to: /opt/anihpj/                                           │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     ✅ Downloaded 45 MB, extracted to /opt/anihpj/                      │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │                                                                        │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  Step 3: BeforeInstall (10 sec)                                        │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  └─ Run: scripts/before_install.sh                                     │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ Backup current version: /opt/anihpj-v2.2.bak                   │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ Clear old files: rm -rf /opt/anihpj/*                          │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     ✅ Backup created, old files removed                               │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │                                                                        │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  Step 4: Install (1 min)                                               │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  └─ Run: scripts/install_dependencies.sh                               │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ cd /opt/anihpj                                                  │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ pip install -r requirements.txt                                 │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ python manage.py collectstatic --no-input                       │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     ✅ Dependencies installed, static files collected                  │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │                                                                        │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  Step 5: AfterInstall (30 sec)                                         │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  └─ Run: scripts/after_install.sh                                      │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ chown -R ubuntu:ubuntu /opt/anihpj                             │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ chmod +x /opt/anihpj/manage.py                                  │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ Setup log directory: mkdir -p /var/log/anihpj                  │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     ✅ Permissions set, directories created                            │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │                                                                        │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  Step 6: ApplicationStart (45 sec)                                     │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  └─ Run: scripts/start_server.sh                                       │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ python manage.py migrate --no-input                            │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ sudo systemctl start gunicorn                                   │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ sudo systemctl start nginx                                      │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     ✅ Migrations applied, servers started                             │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │                                                                        │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  Step 7: ValidateService (30 sec)                                      │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  └─ Run: scripts/validate_service.sh                                   │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ curl http://localhost:8000/api/health/                          │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     └─ Response: {"status": "healthy", "version": "2.3"}              │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     ✅ Health check passed                                             │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │                                                                        │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  Step 8: RegisterWithELB (10 sec)                                      │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  └─ Add instance back to ALB target group                             │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │  └─ Wait for health checks (3 consecutive successes)                  │ │ │ │
│  │  │  │     ✅ Instance registered, receiving traffic                          │ │ │ │
│  │  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │
│  │  │                                                                              │ │ │
│  │  │  ✅ Instance 1 Deployment: SUCCESS (3 minutes 45 seconds)                   │ │ │
│  │  │  └─ Version: 2.2 → 2.3                                                       │ │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │  │  🟢 PHASE 2: Deploy to Instance 2 (us-east-1b)                               │ │ │
│  │  │  └─ Same lifecycle hooks as Phase 1                                          │ │ │
│  │  │  ✅ Instance 2 Deployment: SUCCESS (3 minutes 45 seconds)                    │ │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │  │  🟣 PHASE 3: Deploy to Instance 3 (us-east-1c)                               │ │ │
│  │  │  └─ Same lifecycle hooks as Phase 1                                          │ │ │
│  │  │  ✅ Instance 3 Deployment: SUCCESS (3 minutes 45 seconds)                    │ │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  Total Deployment Time: 11 minutes 15 seconds                                     │ │
│  │  Zero Downtime: ALB always had 2 healthy instances serving traffic                │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                         │                                               │
│                                         ▼                                               │
│  ✅ DEPLOYMENT RESULT                                                                   │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  Status: ✅ DEPLOYMENT SUCCEEDED                                                    │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  Summary:                                                                          │ │
│  │  ├─ Deployment ID: d-ABC123XYZ                                                     │ │
│  │  ├─ Revision: anihpj-v2.3 (GitHub: commit sha123abc)                              │ │
│  │  ├─ Instances: 3/3 succeeded                                                       │ │
│  │  ├─ Duration: 11 min 15 sec                                                        │ │
│  │  ├─ Downtime: 0 seconds (zero-downtime rolling update)                            │ │
│  │  ├─ Rollback: Not required                                                         │ │
│  │  └─ Cost: $0.00 (EC2 deployments are free)                                         │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  CloudWatch Logs:                                                                  │ │
│  │  └─ /aws/codedeploy/anihpj-jobportal                                               │ │
│  │     ├─ Deployment logs (all lifecycle hooks)                                       │ │
│  │     ├─ Script outputs (stdout/stderr)                                              │ │
│  │     └─ Error logs (if any failures)                                                │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  SNS Notification Sent:                                                            │ │
│  │  └─ To: dev-team@anihpj.com                                                        │ │
│  │     Subject: ✅ CodeDeploy SUCCESS: anihpj-v2.3 deployed to production             │ │
│  │     Body: All 3 instances updated successfully in 11 min                           │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                          │
│  📊 MONITORING & VALIDATION                                                              │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  CloudWatch Alarms (monitoring for 10 minutes post-deploy):                       │ │
│  │  ├─ ✅ CPU Utilization < 80% (currently 45%)                                        │ │
│  │  ├─ ✅ 5xx Errors < 10/min (currently 0)                                            │ │
│  │  ├─ ✅ Response Time < 500ms (currently 180ms)                                      │ │
│  │  └─ ✅ Healthy Host Count = 3 (all healthy)                                         │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  Application Health:                                                               │ │
│  │  └─ GET https://anihpj.com/api/health/                                             │ │
│  │     Response 200: {"status": "healthy", "version": "2.3", "db": "connected"}      │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘


┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         🚨 ROLLBACK SCENARIO (If Deployment Fails)                       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                          │
│  Trigger: CloudWatch alarm fires (5xx errors > 10) during Phase 2                       │
│                                                                                          │
│  Automatic Rollback Process:                                                             │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  1️⃣ Stop Deployment (immediately)                                                   │ │
│  │     └─ Cancel Phase 3 deployment                                                    │ │
│  │     └─ Instance 1-2: Bad version (2.3)                                              │ │
│  │     └─ Instance 3: Good version (2.2) ← Still serving traffic                       │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  2️⃣ Create Rollback Deployment (30 seconds)                                         │ │
│  │     └─ Source: Previous revision (anihpj-v2.2)                                      │ │
│  │     └─ Target: Instances 1-2 (failed instances)                                     │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  3️⃣ Execute Rollback (6 minutes)                                                    │ │
│  │     └─ Deploy v2.2 to Instance 1 (3 min)                                            │ │
│  │     └─ Deploy v2.2 to Instance 2 (3 min)                                            │ │
│  │     └─ All instances now on v2.2 (stable version)                                   │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  4️⃣ Validation (1 minute)                                                           │ │
│  │     └─ Health checks pass on all instances                                          │ │
│  │     └─ CloudWatch alarms return to normal                                           │ │
│  │     └─ Application stable                                                           │ │
│  │                                                                                    │ │
│  │  ✅ Rollback Complete: 7 minutes from alarm trigger                                 │ │
│  │  Total Impact: 2 instances had bad code for 7 minutes (ALB still had 1 healthy)    │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                          │
│  SNS Notification:                                                                       │
│  └─ Subject: 🚨 CodeDeploy ROLLBACK: anihpj-v2.3 deployment failed                      │
│     Body: Detected high 5xx errors. Automatically rolled back to v2.2.                  │
│           Investigate logs: /aws/codedeploy/anihpj-jobportal                            │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🔧 2. Core Concepts

Understanding CodeDeploy's core components is essential for effective deployment automation. Let's explore the key concepts that make up a CodeDeploy deployment.

📦 1. CodeDeploy Application

A CodeDeploy Application is a logical container that organizes your deployment components. It uniquely identifies the application you want to deploy and groups related deployment configurations.

anihpj Example: 
# Create CodeDeploy application for anihpj
aws deploy create-application \
    --application-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --compute-platform Server

# Output:
# {
#     "applicationId": "12345678-abcd-efgh-ijkl-1234567890ab"
# }
💡 Best Practice: Create separate applications for different environments (dev, staging, prod) or use deployment groups to separate them. For anihpj, use one application with multiple deployment groups.
🎯 2. Deployment Group

A Deployment Group specifies the target instances for your deployment. It defines which EC2 instances, Lambda functions, or ECS services will receive the deployment.

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              DEPLOYMENT GROUP COMPONENTS                       │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                │
│  1️⃣ Target Selection                                           │
│     ├─ EC2 Tag Filtering (e.g., App=anihpj, Env=prod)         │
│     ├─ Auto Scaling Group (automatic instance discovery)       │
│     └─ On-premises instances (by tag)                          │
│                                                                │
│  2️⃣ Deployment Configuration                                   │
│     ├─ OneAtATime (deploy to 1 instance at a time)            │
│     ├─ HalfAtATime (50% concurrency)                          │
│     ├─ AllAtOnce (all instances simultaneously)               │
│     └─ Custom (e.g., 25% at a time)                           │
│                                                                │
│  3️⃣ Load Balancer Integration                                 │
│     ├─ Application Load Balancer (ALB)                        │
│     ├─ Network Load Balancer (NLB)                            │
│     ├─ Classic Load Balancer (CLB)                            │
│     └─ Deregister → Deploy → Re-register                      │
│                                                                │
│  4️⃣ Rollback Configuration                                     │
│     ├─ Automatic rollback on deployment failure               │
│     ├─ Automatic rollback on CloudWatch alarm                 │
│     └─ Manual rollback on demand                              │
│                                                                │
│  5️⃣ Triggers & Notifications                                   │
│     ├─ SNS topic for deployment events                        │
│     ├─ CloudWatch Events integration                          │
│     └─ Email/SMS notifications                                │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
anihpj Production Deployment Group: 
# Create deployment group for anihpj production
aws deploy create-deployment-group \
    --application-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --deployment-group-name anihpj-prod-dg \
    --deployment-config-name CodeDeployDefault.OneAtATime \
    --ec2-tag-filters Key=App,Value=anihpj,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE Key=Env,Value=prod,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
    --service-role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployServiceRole \
    --load-balancer-info targetGroupInfoList=[{name=anihpj-tg}] \
    --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM \
    --alarm-configuration alarms=[{name=anihpj-cpu-alarm},{name=anihpj-5xx-alarm}]
📝 3. Application Revision

A Revision is a specific version of your deployable content (application code + AppSpec file). Revisions are stored in S3, GitHub, or Bitbucket.

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   REVISION STRUCTURE                           │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                │
│  anihpj-v2.3.zip (S3) or GitHub Commit                        │
│  ├─ appspec.yml          ← Required: Deployment instructions  │
│  ├─ anihpj/                                                    │
│  │  ├─ __init__.py                                            │
│  │  ├─ settings.py                                            │
│  │  ├─ urls.py                                                │
│  │  └─ wsgi.py                                                │
│  ├─ jobpost/                                                   │
│  │  ├─ models.py                                              │
│  │  ├─ views.py                                               │
│  │  └─ urls.py                                                │
│  ├─ scripts/             ← Lifecycle event hooks              │
│  │  ├─ stop_server.sh                                         │
│  │  ├─ before_install.sh                                      │
│  │  ├─ install_dependencies.sh                                │
│  │  ├─ after_install.sh                                       │
│  │  ├─ start_server.sh                                        │
│  │  └─ validate_service.sh                                    │
│  ├─ requirements.txt                                           │
│  ├─ manage.py                                                  │
│  └─ static/                                                    │
│     └─ (CSS, JS, images)                                       │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Registering a Revision from S3: 
# Upload revision to S3
aws s3 cp anihpj-v2.3.zip s3://anihpj-deployments/

# Register revision
aws deploy register-application-revision \
    --application-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --s3-location bucket=anihpj-deployments,key=anihpj-v2.3.zip,bundleType=zip \
    --description "anihpj Django v2.3 - Added job alerts feature"
Using GitHub as Source: 
# Deploy directly from GitHub
aws deploy create-deployment \
    --application-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --deployment-group-name anihpj-prod-dg \
    --github-location repository=yourusername/anihpj,commitId=abc123def456
📄 4. AppSpec File (appspec.yml)

The AppSpec file is the heart of CodeDeploy. It defines how to deploy your application, including file mappings, permissions, and lifecycle event hooks.

# appspec.yml for anihpj Django deployment
version: 0.0
os: linux

files:
  # Source → Destination mapping
  - source: /
    destination: /opt/anihpj
  - source: /scripts
    destination: /opt/anihpj/scripts

permissions:
  # Set ownership and permissions
  - object: /opt/anihpj
    owner: ubuntu
    group: ubuntu
    mode: 755
    type:
      - directory
  - object: /opt/anihpj/manage.py
    owner: ubuntu
    group: ubuntu
    mode: 755
    type:
      - file

hooks:
  # Lifecycle event hooks (run in this order)
  
  ApplicationStop:
    - location: scripts/stop_server.sh
      timeout: 60
      runas: root
  
  DownloadBundle:
    # CodeDeploy handles this automatically
  
  BeforeInstall:
    - location: scripts/before_install.sh
      timeout: 300
      runas: root
  
  Install:
    # CodeDeploy copies files based on 'files' section
  
  AfterInstall:
    - location: scripts/after_install.sh
      timeout: 300
      runas: ubuntu
  
  ApplicationStart:
    - location: scripts/start_server.sh
      timeout: 300
      runas: root
  
  ValidateService:
    - location: scripts/validate_service.sh
      timeout: 120
      runas: ubuntu
⚠️ Important: The AppSpec file must be named appspec.yml (or appspec.json) and placed in the root of your revision. CodeDeploy will fail without it.
🔄 5. Deployment Lifecycle Events

CodeDeploy executes your deployment in a series of lifecycle events. Each event can trigger custom scripts (hooks) that you define.

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    EC2/ON-PREMISES LIFECYCLE EVENTS                        │
│                      (Executed in this exact order)                        │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                            │
│  1️⃣ ApplicationStop                                                        │
│     Purpose: Stop the currently running application                       │
│     anihpj: Stop Gunicorn and Nginx                                       │
│     Runs on: EXISTING instance (before new code arrives)                  │
│     ⏱️ Typical duration: 30 seconds                                        │
│                                                                            │
│  2️⃣ DownloadBundle                                                         │
│     Purpose: Download revision from S3/GitHub                             │
│     anihpj: Download anihpj-v2.3.zip from S3                              │
│     Managed by: CodeDeploy (automatic, no hook needed)                    │
│     ⏱️ Typical duration: 20 seconds (depends on bundle size)              │
│                                                                            │
│  3️⃣ BeforeInstall                                                          │
│     Purpose: Pre-installation tasks (backup, cleanup)                     │
│     anihpj: Backup current version, clear old files                       │
│     Use case: Create rollback point, prepare directories                  │
│     ⏱️ Typical duration: 10 seconds                                        │
│                                                                            │
│  4️⃣ Install                                                                │
│     Purpose: Copy files from revision to destination                      │
│     anihpj: Copy to /opt/anihpj, apply permissions                        │
│     Managed by: CodeDeploy (based on 'files' in appspec.yml)              │
│     ⏱️ Typical duration: 10 seconds                                        │
│                                                                            │
│  5️⃣ AfterInstall                                                           │
│     Purpose: Post-installation tasks                                      │
│     anihpj: pip install, collectstatic, setup directories                 │
│     Use case: Install dependencies, configure environment                 │
│     ⏱️ Typical duration: 60 seconds                                        │
│                                                                            │
│  6️⃣ ApplicationStart                                                       │
│     Purpose: Start the application                                        │
│     anihpj: Run migrations, start Gunicorn and Nginx                      │
│     Critical: Database migrations happen here                             │
│     ⏱️ Typical duration: 45 seconds                                        │
│                                                                            │
│  7️⃣ ValidateService                                                        │
│     Purpose: Verify application is running correctly                      │
│     anihpj: Health check /api/health/, test database connection           │
│     Triggers rollback: If validation fails                                │
│     ⏱️ Typical duration: 30 seconds                                        │
│                                                                            │
│  8️⃣ (Optional) BeforeBlockTraffic / AfterBlockTraffic                      │
│     Purpose: Execute scripts before/after removing from load balancer     │
│     anihpj: Usually not needed (use ApplicationStop instead)              │
│                                                                            │
│  9️⃣ (Optional) BeforeAllowTraffic / AfterAllowTraffic                      │
│     Purpose: Execute scripts before/after adding to load balancer         │
│     anihpj: Warm up cache, pre-load data                                  │
│                                                                            │
│  Total Deployment Time per Instance: ~3-4 minutes                         │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
⏱️ Deployment Timeline Visualization
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        🕐 DEPLOYMENT LIFECYCLE - VISUAL TIMELINE                                  │
│                            (anihpj Django - Single EC2 Instance)                                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TIME        EVENT                      ACTION                              STATUS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
00:00       ╔════════════════════╗
            ║  DEPLOYMENT START  ║     CodeDeploy receives trigger       🟡 Starting
            ╚════════════════════╝
                     │
00:05       ├─ 1️⃣ ApplicationStop       🛑 Stop Gunicorn/Apache             🔴 App Offline
            │       │
            │       ├── sudo systemctl stop gunicorn
            │       ├── sudo systemctl stop apache2
            │       └── Log current PID for rollback
            │
00:35       ├─ 2️⃣ DownloadBundle        📥 Download from S3                 🔴 Downloading
            │       │
            │       ├── Fetch: s3://anihpj-artifacts/v1.2.3.zip
            │       ├── Extract to /opt/codedeploy-agent/deployment-root
            │       └── Verify checksum
            │
00:55       ├─ 3️⃣ BeforeInstall         🗂️  Backup & Prepare                🔴 Preparing
            │       │
            │       ├── cp -r /opt/anihpj /opt/anihpj.backup.$(date +%s)
            │       ├── rm -rf /opt/anihpj/staticfiles/*
            │       └── mkdir -p /opt/anihpj/logs
            │
01:05       ├─ 4️⃣ Install               📦 Copy Files                       🔴 Installing
            │       │
            │       ├── Copy from deployment-root to /opt/anihpj
            │       ├── Set owner: chown www-data:www-data
            │       └── Set permissions: chmod 755
            │
01:15       ├─ 5️⃣ AfterInstall          🔧 Dependencies & Config            🔴 Configuring
            │       │
            │       ├── cd /opt/anihpj
            │       ├── pip install -r requirements.txt
            │       ├── python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
            │       ├── cp .env.production .env
            │       └── Update settings.py configurations
            │
02:15       ├─ 6️⃣ ApplicationStart      🚀 Start Services & Migrate         🟡 Starting
            │       │
            │       ├── python manage.py migrate --noinput
            │       ├── python manage.py createcachetable
            │       ├── sudo systemctl start gunicorn
            │       ├── sudo systemctl start apache2
            │       └── Wait for service to be ready (10s)
            │
03:00       ├─ 7️⃣ ValidateService       ✅ Health Checks                    🟢 Validating
            │       │
            │       ├── curl http://localhost:8000/health
            │       ├── Check HTTP 200 OK response
            │       ├── Verify database connection
            │       ├── Check static files serving
            │       └── Test sample job listing page
            │
03:30       ╔════════════════════╗
            ║  DEPLOYMENT        ║     All instances updated              ✅ SUCCESS
            ║  SUCCESSFUL        ║     Application running                🟢 App Online
            ╚════════════════════╝


┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          ⚙️ ROLLING DEPLOYMENT TIMELINE (3 Instances)                             │
│                               Strategy: OneAtATime                                                │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

INSTANCE     00:00      03:30      07:00      10:30
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
EC2-1        🟢 v1.2.2  🔴 Deploy  ✅ v1.2.3  ✅ v1.2.3
(i-abc123)   ██████████ ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓ ██████████ ██████████
             Online     Updating   Online     Online

EC2-2        🟢 v1.2.2  🟢 v1.2.2  🔴 Deploy  ✅ v1.2.3
(i-def456)   ██████████ ██████████ ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓ ██████████
             Online     Online     Updating   Online

EC2-3        🟢 v1.2.2  🟢 v1.2.2  🟢 v1.2.2  🔴 Deploy  ✅ v1.2.3
(i-ghi789)   ██████████ ██████████ ██████████ ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓ ██████████
             Online     Online     Online     Updating   Online

CAPACITY     3/3        2/3        2/3        2/3        3/3
             100%       67%        67%        67%        100%

TRAFFIC      ← All users served throughout deployment (no complete downtime)


┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        🎯 TIMING BREAKDOWN BY STRATEGY                                            │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────┬──────────────────┬──────────────────┬──────────────────┬──────────────────┐
│ Strategy            │ Per Instance     │ Total Time       │ Downtime         │ Rollback Time    │
├─────────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┤
│ 🔵🟢 Blue/Green       │ 3-4 minutes      │ 3-4 minutes      │ 0 seconds        │ 30-60 seconds    │
│ (ALB + New Fleet)   │ (parallel)       │ (all at once)    │ (traffic switch) │ (instant switch) │
├─────────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┤
│ 🔄 Rolling          │ 3-4 minutes      │ 9-12 minutes     │ 0 seconds        │ 2-5 minutes      │
│ (OneAtATime)        │ (sequential)     │ (3 instances)    │ (partial avail)  │ (reverse roll)   │
├─────────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┤
│ 📍 In-Place         │ 3-4 minutes      │ 3-4 minutes      │ 3-4 minutes      │ 5-10 minutes     │
│ (AllAtOnce)         │ (parallel)       │ (all together)   │ (full outage)    │ (redeploy old)   │
├─────────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┼──────────────────┤
│ 🐤 Canary           │ 3-4 minutes      │ 5-8 minutes      │ 0 seconds        │ 30-60 seconds    │
│ (10% then 90%)      │ (per batch)      │ (staged shift)   │ (traffic shift)  │ (instant switch) │
└─────────────────────┴──────────────────┴──────────────────┴──────────────────┴──────────────────┘


┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              🚨 ROLLBACK TRIGGER SCENARIOS                                        │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

SCENARIO                          DETECTION TIME    ROLLBACK TIME    TOTAL DOWNTIME
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
❌ ValidateService fails            30 seconds        1-2 minutes      1.5-2.5 minutes
   (Health check returns 500)

❌ CloudWatch alarm triggers         2 minutes         1-2 minutes      3-4 minutes
   (Error rate > 5%)

❌ Manual rollback initiated         Immediate         1-2 minutes      1-2 minutes
   (Admin detects issue)

✅ Successful deployment             N/A               N/A              0 seconds
   (All checks pass)
⏱️ anihpj Production Timing:
• Blue/Green: 4 minutes total, 0 downtime, instant rollback ← RECOMMENDED
• Rolling (OneAtATime): 12 minutes total, 67% capacity maintained during deployment
• Average Deployment: Weekly release takes 4 minutes, typically runs Friday 6 PM EST
• Validation Time: 30 seconds health check ensures no bad deployments reach users
Example Hook Scripts for anihpj: scripts/stop_server.sh 
#!/bin/bash
# ApplicationStop: Stop Gunicorn and Nginx

echo "Stopping Gunicorn..."
sudo systemctl stop gunicorn

echo "Stopping Nginx..."
sudo systemctl stop nginx

echo "Services stopped successfully"
exit 0
scripts/after_install.sh 
#!/bin/bash
# AfterInstall: Install dependencies and collect static files

cd /opt/anihpj

echo "Installing Python dependencies..."
pip3 install -r requirements.txt

echo "Collecting static files..."
python3 manage.py collectstatic --no-input

echo "Setting up log directory..."
mkdir -p /var/log/anihpj
chown ubuntu:ubuntu /var/log/anihpj

echo "Dependencies installed successfully"
exit 0
scripts/start_server.sh 
#!/bin/bash
# ApplicationStart: Run migrations and start services

cd /opt/anihpj

echo "Running database migrations..."
python3 manage.py migrate --no-input

echo "Starting Gunicorn..."
sudo systemctl start gunicorn

echo "Starting Nginx..."
sudo systemctl start nginx

sleep 5  # Wait for services to start

echo "Services started successfully"
exit 0
scripts/validate_service.sh 
#!/bin/bash
# ValidateService: Health check

cd /opt/anihpj

echo "Checking application health..."
response=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8000/api/health/)

if [ "$response" == "200" ]; then
    echo "Health check passed: HTTP 200"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Health check failed: HTTP $response"
    exit 1  # This will trigger rollback
fi
⚙️ 6. Deployment Configuration

A Deployment Configuration determines how fast CodeDeploy deploys your application (how many instances at a time).

Configuration Strategy Use Case anihpj Recommendation
CodeDeployDefault.OneAtATime Deploy to 1 instance, wait for success, move to next Maximum safety, slowest speed ✅ RECOMMENDED for production (3 instances)
CodeDeployDefault.HalfAtATime Deploy to 50% of instances simultaneously Balanced speed and safety ⚠️ Use if you have 6+ instances
CodeDeployDefault.AllAtOnce Deploy to all instances simultaneously Fastest, but risky (possible downtime) ❌ NOT recommended (no safety net)
Custom (e.g., 25%) Deploy to custom percentage at a time Large fleets (20+ instances) 💡 Create custom: 10 instances at a time
Creating Custom Deployment Configuration: 
# Create custom config: Deploy to 2 instances at a time
aws deploy create-deployment-config \
    --deployment-config-name anihpj-TwoAtATime \
    --minimum-healthy-hosts type=FLEET_PERCENT,value=67 \
    --compute-platform Server

# This ensures at least 67% of instances remain healthy
# For 3 instances: At least 2 must be healthy (can deploy to 1 at a time)
🤖 7. CodeDeploy Agent

The CodeDeploy Agent is a software package that runs on your EC2 instances and on-premises servers. It communicates with CodeDeploy to execute deployments.

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 CODEDEPLOY AGENT WORKFLOW                      │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                │
│  EC2 Instance                                                  │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  CodeDeploy Agent (daemon process)                       │ │
│  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │ │
│  │  │  1. Poll CodeDeploy Service (every 30 seconds)     │  │ │
│  │  │     "Any deployments for me?"                      │  │ │
│  │  │                                                     │  │ │
│  │  │  2. Receive deployment instructions                │  │ │
│  │  │     Download location: s3://anihpj-deployments/    │  │ │
│  │  │     AppSpec location: root/appspec.yml             │  │ │
│  │  │                                                     │  │ │
│  │  │  3. Download revision from S3                      │  │ │
│  │  │     Extract to: /opt/codedeploy-agent/deployment-root/ │ │
│  │  │                                                     │  │ │
│  │  │  4. Execute lifecycle hooks                        │  │ │
│  │  │     Run scripts in order (ApplicationStop → ...)   │  │ │
│  │  │     Capture stdout/stderr                          │  │ │
│  │  │     Monitor exit codes (0 = success, !0 = fail)    │  │ │
│  │  │                                                     │  │ │
│  │  │  5. Report status to CodeDeploy                    │  │ │
│  │  │     Success/Failure for each lifecycle event       │  │ │
│  │  │     Upload logs to CloudWatch                      │  │ │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                            │                                   │
│                            ▼                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Agent Logs: /var/log/aws/codedeploy-agent/             │ │
│  │  ├─ codedeploy-agent.log (agent activity)               │ │
│  │  └─ deployment-root/ (deployment logs)                  │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Installing CodeDeploy Agent on Ubuntu EC2: 
#!/bin/bash
# Install CodeDeploy agent on Ubuntu 20.04/22.04

# Update system
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y ruby-full wget

# Download and install agent
cd /tmp
wget https://aws-codedeploy-us-east-1.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/latest/install
chmod +x ./install
sudo ./install auto

# Start and enable agent
sudo systemctl start codedeploy-agent
sudo systemctl enable codedeploy-agent

# Verify agent is running
sudo systemctl status codedeploy-agent

# Expected output: active (running)
Checking Agent Status: 
# Check if agent is running
sudo systemctl status codedeploy-agent

# View agent logs
sudo tail -f /var/log/aws/codedeploy-agent/codedeploy-agent.log

# Restart agent if needed
sudo systemctl restart codedeploy-agent
✅ Pro Tip: Add CodeDeploy agent installation to your EC2 user data script, so new instances automatically have the agent: 
#!/bin/bash
# EC2 User Data for anihpj instances
apt-get update
apt-get install -y ruby-full wget

# Install CodeDeploy agent
cd /tmp
wget https://aws-codedeploy-us-east-1.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/latest/install
chmod +x ./install
./install auto
systemctl start codedeploy-agent
systemctl enable codedeploy-agent

🚀 3. Getting Started with CodeDeploy for Django

Let's deploy the anihpj Django application using CodeDeploy with a complete step-by-step guide. This will set up zero-downtime deployments to EC2 instances.

🎯 What We'll Build:
  • CodeDeploy application and deployment group
  • 3 EC2 instances with CodeDeploy agent
  • Rolling deployment (OneAtATime) with ALB integration
  • Automatic rollback on failure
  • Complete appspec.yml with lifecycle hooks
⏱️ Total Setup Time: 45 minutes
📋 Step 1: Prerequisites

Before starting, ensure you have:

  • ✅ AWS account with appropriate permissions
  • ✅ 3 EC2 instances running Ubuntu 20.04/22.04 (or Amazon Linux 2023)
  • ✅ Application Load Balancer (ALB) configured
  • ✅ Django application in GitHub or S3
  • ✅ AWS CLI installed and configured
🔐 Step 2: Create IAM Roles
# Create CodeDeploy Service Role
aws iam create-role \
    --role-name CodeDeployServiceRole \
    --assume-role-policy-document '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Allow","Principal":{"Service":"codedeploy.amazonaws.com"},"Action":"sts:AssumeRole"}]}'

aws iam attach-role-policy \
    --role-name CodeDeployServiceRole \
    --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AWSCodeDeployRole
📦 Step 3: Create CodeDeploy Application
aws deploy create-application \
    --application-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --compute-platform Server
🎯 Step 4: Create Deployment Group
aws deploy create-deployment-group \
    --application-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --deployment-group-name anihpj-prod-dg \
    --deployment-config-name CodeDeployDefault.OneAtATime \
    --ec2-tag-filters Key=App,Value=anihpj,Type=KEY_AND_VALUE \
    --service-role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployServiceRole \
    --load-balancer-info targetGroupInfoList=[{name=anihpj-tg}] \
    --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE
🚀 Step 5: Deploy from GitHub
aws deploy create-deployment \
    --application-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --deployment-group-name anihpj-prod-dg \
    --github-location repository=yourusername/anihpj,commitId=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
🎉 Congratulations! CodeDeploy is Now Set Up

Next Steps: Integrate with CodePipeline, set up CloudWatch alarms, configure SNS notifications

🔑 4. Key Features & Components

1. Deployment Types
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              IN-PLACE vs BLUE/GREEN DEPLOYMENTS             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  📍 IN-PLACE DEPLOYMENT                                      │
│  ├─ Updates existing instances                             │
│  ├─ Application stops → New version installs → Starts      │
│  ├─ Use with: EC2, on-premises                             │
│  ├─ Best for: Cost savings (no extra instances)            │
│  └─ anihpj: Rolling update 3 instances (OneAtATime)        │
│                                                             │
│  🔵🟢 BLUE/GREEN DEPLOYMENT                                   │
│  ├─ Creates new environment (green) alongside old (blue)   │
│  ├─ Traffic switches to green after validation            │
│  ├─ Blue environment kept for rollback                     │
│  ├─ Use with: EC2 + ALB, Lambda, ECS                       │
│  ├─ Best for: Zero-downtime, instant rollback              │
│  └─ anihpj: Duplicate 3 instances → Test → Switch traffic  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📊 Deployment Strategy Comparison
Strategy Downtime Rollback Speed Resource Cost Risk Level Best For
🔵🟢 Blue/Green Zero ⚡ Instant (1-2 min) 💰💰 High
(2x instances) 		Lowest Production apps, critical services, anihpj Django with ALB
🔄 Rolling Partial ⏱️ Fast (2-5 min) 💰 Low
(same instances) 		Medium Cost-conscious deployments, gradual updates
📍 In-Place (AllAtOnce) Yes (5-10 min) 🐢 Slow (10-15 min) 💰 Lowest
(same instances) 		High Dev/test environments, non-critical services
🐤 Canary Zero ⚡ Instant (1-2 min) 💰💰 Medium
(1.2-1.5x instances) 		Very Low Testing new features with small user subset
🔨 Manual Yes (30+ min) 🐢 Very Slow (30+ min) 💰 Low
(labor costs high) 		Highest Legacy systems (not recommended)
💡 anihpj Recommendation: Use Blue/Green with ALB for production (zero downtime), Rolling for staging (cost-effective), and AllAtOnce for development (fastest updates).
2. Traffic Shifting Strategies (Blue/Green)
Strategy Description Use Case
All-at-once Switch 100% traffic immediately Small applications, low risk
Canary Shift 10%, wait, then 90% Test with subset of users
Linear Gradual shift (10% every N minutes) anihpj production (safe rollout)
3. Automatic Rollback
# Configure automatic rollback on alarm
aws deploy update-deployment-group \
    --application-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --current-deployment-group-name anihpj-prod-dg \
    --auto-rollback-configuration enabled=true,events=DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE,DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM \
    --alarm-configuration alarms=[{name=anihpj-cpu-high},{name=anihpj-5xx-errors}]
4. Deployment Triggers & Notifications
# Add SNS trigger for deployment events
aws deploy put-lifecycle-event-hook-execution-status \
    --deployment-id d-ABC123XYZ \
    --lifecycle-event-hook-execution-id hook-123 \
    --status Succeeded

📈 6. Scalability & High Availability

🔄 Integration with Auto Scaling

CodeDeploy automatically discovers new EC2 instances launched by Auto Scaling and deploys the latest application revision.

# Auto Scaling launches new instance
# CodeDeploy agent polls for deployment instructions
# Automatically deploys latest anihpj revision
# Instance joins ALB target group after successful deployment
🌍 Multi-Region Deployments

Deploy anihpj to multiple AWS regions for global availability:

  • Create CodeDeploy application in each region
  • Use AWS CodePipeline for coordinated multi-region deployments
  • Route 53 health checks for automatic failover

🎓 7. Advanced Topics

🔵🟢 Blue/Green Deployment Setup
# Create blue/green deployment
aws deploy create-deployment \
    --application-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --deployment-group-name anihpj-bluegreen-dg \
    --deployment-config-name CodeDeployDefault.LambdaLinear10PercentEvery1Minute \
    --description "Blue/green deployment for anihpj v2.0"
⚡ Lambda Function Deployments
# appspec.yml for Lambda
version: 0.0
Resources:
  - anihpjProcessorFunction:
      Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
      Properties:
        Name: "anihpj-job-processor"
        Alias: "prod"
        CurrentVersion: "1"
        TargetVersion: "2"
🐳 ECS Container Deployments

Deploy Docker-based anihpj with CodeDeploy + ECS blue/green strategy.

🔧 Custom Lifecycle Hooks
# scripts/custom_validation.sh
#!/bin/bash
# Run load test before allowing traffic
ab -n 1000 -c 10 http://localhost:8000/
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then exit 0; else exit 1; fi

✨ 8. Best Practices

🔒 Security Checklist
  • ✅ Use IAM roles (never hardcode credentials)
  • ✅ Enable CloudTrail logging for all CodeDeploy actions
  • ✅ Encrypt S3 buckets containing deployment artifacts
  • ✅ Restrict CodeDeploy service role permissions (least privilege)
  • ✅ Use VPC endpoints for private deployments
  • ✅ Enable MFA for deployment approvals
💻 Deployment Best Practices
  • ✅ Always use OneAtATime for production (anihpj)
  • ✅ Set appropriate timeouts (allow for migrations)
  • ✅ Create backups in BeforeInstall hook
  • ✅ Run database migrations in ApplicationStart
  • ✅ Implement comprehensive health checks in ValidateService
  • ✅ Configure automatic rollback on CloudWatch alarms
  • ✅ Tag all deployments with version numbers
  • ✅ Keep appspec.yml in version control
🧪 Testing Strategy
  • ✅ Test appspec.yml in development environment first
  • ✅ Validate all lifecycle hooks locally
  • ✅ Run smoke tests in ValidateService hook
  • ✅ Monitor CloudWatch metrics for 10 minutes post-deployment
📊 Monitoring & Logging
# CloudWatch Log Groups to monitor
/aws/codedeploy/anihpj-jobportal  # Deployment logs
/var/log/aws/codedeploy-agent/    # Agent logs on EC2

# Key CloudWatch Metrics
DeploymentSuccessful
DeploymentFailed  
DeploymentDuration

💼 9. Common Use Cases

🐍 Django Application Deployment (anihpj)

Scenario: Zero-downtime deployment of Django job portal with database migrations

Deployment Flow:
1. ApplicationStop: Stop Gunicorn
2. BeforeInstall: Backup database, backup code
3. Install: Copy new code to /opt/anihpj
4. AfterInstall: pip install requirements
5. ApplicationStart: Run migrations, start Gunicorn
6. ValidateService: Health check /api/health/
7. Traffic resumes to instance

Result: 3-instance fleet updated one at a time, zero downtime
🔧 Microservices Deployment

Deploy multiple services independently with coordinated rollouts

🧪 A/B Testing & Feature Flags

Deploy new features to subset of servers using canary deployments

🔧 10. Troubleshooting

⚠️ Common Issues & Solutions
Issue Cause Solution
Agent not running Agent crashed or not installed sudo systemctl restart codedeploy-agent
Deployment fails at ApplicationStop Script errors or permission issues Check script with bash -x script.sh
Permission denied Wrong runas user in appspec.yml Use runas: root for system commands
Health check fails Application not fully started Add sleep 10 in ValidateService
🐛 Debugging Deployments
# View deployment logs
aws deploy get-deployment --deployment-id d-ABC123XYZ

# SSH to instance and check agent logs
sudo tail -f /var/log/aws/codedeploy-agent/codedeploy-agent.log

# Check specific deployment logs on instance
sudo ls /opt/codedeploy-agent/deployment-root/
sudo cat /opt/codedeploy-agent/deployment-root/[deployment-id]/logs/scripts.log
❓ Frequently Asked Questions
Q: Can I roll back a deployment?

Yes! Use aws deploy stop-deployment or enable automatic rollback.

Q: How do I deploy to specific instances?

Use EC2 tags to target specific instances in your deployment group.

Q: Can I schedule deployments?

Yes! Use EventBridge rules to trigger CodeDeploy at specific times.

⌨️ 11. API & CLI Reference

🚀 Essential AWS CLI Commands
# List applications
aws deploy list-applications

# List deployment groups
aws deploy list-deployment-groups --application-name anihpj-jobportal

# Create deployment
aws deploy create-deployment \
    --application-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --deployment-group-name anihpj-prod-dg \
    --s3-location bucket=anihpj-deployments,key=anihpj-v2.0.zip,bundleType=zip

# Stop deployment
aws deploy stop-deployment --deployment-id d-ABC123XYZ --auto-rollback-enabled

# List recent deployments
aws deploy list-deployments \
    --application-name anihpj-jobportal \
    --max-items 10 \
    --include-only-statuses Succeeded Failed

# Get deployment status
aws deploy get-deployment --deployment-id d-ABC123XYZ
🐍 Python Boto3 SDK
# anihpj/deploy.py - Automated deployment script
import boto3
from datetime import datetime

codedeploy = boto3.client('codedeploy')

def deploy_anihpj(version):
    """Deploy anihpj Django application"""
    
    response = codedeploy.create_deployment(
        applicationName='anihpj-jobportal',
        deploymentGroupName='anihpj-prod-dg',
        revision={
            's3Location': {
                'bucket': 'anihpj-deployments',
                'key': f'anihpj-{version}.zip',
                'bundleType': 'zip'
            }
        },
        description=f'Deploying anihpj version {version} - {datetime.now()}'
    )
    
    deployment_id = response['deploymentId']
    print(f'✅ Deployment created: {deployment_id}')
    
    # Wait for deployment to complete
    waiter = codedeploy.get_waiter('deployment_successful')
    waiter.wait(deploymentId=deployment_id)
    
    print(f'✅ Deployment {deployment_id} completed successfully!')
    return deployment_id

# Usage
if __name__ == '__main__':
    deploy_anihpj('2.3.0')

🔗 12. AWS Service Integration

🔄 CodePipeline Integration
// Add CodeDeploy as deploy stage in CodePipeline
{
  "name": "Deploy",
  "actions": [{
    "name": "DeployToProduction",
    "actionTypeId": {
      "category": "Deploy",
      "owner": "AWS",
      "provider": "CodeDeploy",
      "version": "1"
    },
    "configuration": {
      "ApplicationName": "anihpj-jobportal",
      "DeploymentGroupName": "anihpj-prod-dg"
    },
    "inputArtifacts": [{"name": "BuildOutput"}]
  }]
}
🏗️ Complete CI/CD Architecture for anihpj Django
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          🚀 ANIHPJ JOB PORTAL - CI/CD PIPELINE ARCHITECTURE                        │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓   ┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓   ┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓   ┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓   ┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
┃   GITHUB     ┃   ┃   CODEPIPELINE   ┃   ┃   CODEBUILD    ┃   ┃   CODEDEPLOY    ┃   ┃   EC2 FLEET  ┃
┃              ┃───▶┃                  ┃───▶┃                ┃───▶┃                 ┃───▶┃              ┃
┃   📦 Repo    ┃   ┃   🔄 Workflow    ┃   ┃   🏗️ Builder  ┃   ┃   🚀 Deployer   ┃   ┃   🖥️ Servers ┃
┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛   ┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛   ┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛   ┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛   ┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛
      │                      │                      │                      │                      │
      │                      │                      │                      │                      │
      ▼                      ▼                      ▼                      ▼                      ▼

┌──────────────┐      ┌──────────────┐      ┌──────────────┐      ┌──────────────┐      ┌──────────────┐
│   1. PUSH    │      │  2. TRIGGER  │      │  3. BUILD    │      │  4. DEPLOY   │      │  5. RUNNING  │
├──────────────┤      ├──────────────┤      ├──────────────┤      ├──────────────┤      ├──────────────┤
│              │      │              │      │              │      │              │      │              │
│ • Developer  │      │ • Webhook    │      │ • Clone repo │      │ • Stop       │      │ • Instance 1 │
│   commits    │      │   triggered  │      │ • Install    │      │   Apache     │      │   ✅ Healthy │
│ • Push to    │      │ • Source     │      │   deps       │      │ • Backup DB  │      │              │
│   main       │      │   stage runs │      │ • Run tests  │      │ • Deploy     │      │ • Instance 2 │
│ • Tag v1.2.3 │      │ • Artifact   │      │ • Collect    │      │   artifact   │      │   ✅ Healthy │
│              │      │   fetched    │      │   static     │      │ • Migrate DB │      │              │
│              │      │              │      │ • Package    │      │ • Start      │      │ • Instance 3 │
│              │      │              │      │ • Upload S3  │      │   Apache     │      │   ✅ Healthy │
│              │      │              │      │ • ✅ Pass   │      │ • Validate   │      │              │
└──────────────┘      └──────────────┘      └──────────────┘      └──────────────┘      └──────────────┘
    ⏱️ 10 sec              ⏱️ 5 sec              ⏱️ 3-5 min            ⏱️ 5-8 min            ⏱️ Live


┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                    🔧 SUPPORTING SERVICES LAYER                                    │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                                   │
│  📦 S3 ARTIFACTS        📊 CLOUDWATCH          📧 SNS              🔐 IAM              🗄️ RDS    │
│  ├─ Build outputs      ├─ Deployment logs     ├─ Success email   ├─ Service roles   ├─ anihpj  │
│  ├─ appspec.yml        ├─ Error metrics       ├─ Failure email   ├─ EC2 profile     │   DB      │
│  ├─ scripts/           ├─ Health metrics      └─ Slack webhook   └─ S3 access       └─ Primary  │
│  └─ Versioned          └─ Alarms (rollback)                                                       │
│                                                                                                   │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘


┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              🎯 DEPLOYMENT FLOW - DETAILED BREAKDOWN                               │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Step 1: SOURCE (GitHub)                          Step 4: DEPLOY (CodeDeploy - Lifecycle Hooks)
├─ Repository: anihpj/jobportal                  ├─ ❌ ApplicationStop (stop_server.sh)
├─ Branch: main                                  │   └─ sudo systemctl stop apache2
├─ Trigger: Push/PR merge                        │
└─ Webhook: Instant notification                 ├─ ⬇️  DownloadBundle (from S3)
                                                  │   └─ Download artifact to /tmp
Step 2: ORCHESTRATE (CodePipeline)               │
├─ Pipeline: anihpj-cicd-pipeline                ├─ 📥 BeforeInstall (before_install.sh)
├─ Stages:                                       │   ├─ sudo apt-get update
│   1. Source (GitHub)                           │   ├─ pip install -r requirements.txt
│   2. Build (CodeBuild)                         │   └─ Backup /opt/anihpj
│   3. Deploy (CodeDeploy)                       │
│   4. [Optional] Approval                       ├─ 📝 Install (CodeDeploy agent)
└─ Artifact Store: s3://anihpj-artifacts/        │   └─ Copy files from /tmp to /opt/anihpj
                                                  │
Step 3: BUILD (CodeBuild)                        ├─ 🔧 AfterInstall (after_install.sh)
├─ Image: python:3.11                            │   ├─ python manage.py collectstatic
├─ buildspec.yml phases:                         │   ├─ sudo chown www-data:www-data -R
│   • Pre-build:                                 │   └─ Set permissions
│     └─ pip install --upgrade pip               │
│   • Build:                                     ├─ 🚀 ApplicationStart (start_server.sh)
│     ├─ python manage.py test                   │   ├─ python manage.py migrate
│     ├─ python manage.py collectstatic          │   └─ sudo systemctl start apache2
│     └─ Create deployment package               │
│   • Post-build:                                └─ ✅ ValidateService (validate_service.sh)
│     └─ Upload to S3                                └─ curl http://localhost:8000/health
└─ Output: anihpj-build-v1.2.3.zip                   └─ Check HTTP 200 OK


Step 5: MONITOR & VALIDATE                       Step 6: ROLLBACK (If Needed)
├─ CloudWatch Alarms:                            ├─ Trigger: Health check fails > 2 min
│   • HTTP 5xx errors > 10                       ├─ Action: Automatic rollback
│   • CPU usage > 80%                            ├─ Process:
│   • Memory usage > 90%                         │   1. Stop current deployment
│   • Response time > 2s                         │   2. Revert to previous revision
└─ Health Check:                                 │   3. Restore old version
    └─ GET /health every 30s                     │   4. Notify team via SNS
                                                  └─ Time: ~2 minutes (Blue/Green)
                                                      └─ ~5 minutes (In-Place Rolling)


┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                   📈 PIPELINE METRICS & STATS                                      │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Total Pipeline Time: 8-14 minutes (source to production)                                        │
│  Success Rate: 98.5% (automated tests catch issues early)                                        │
│  Rollback Time: 1-2 minutes (Blue/Green) | 5-10 minutes (Rolling)                                │
│  Deployments/Month: 20-25 (weekly releases + hotfixes)                                           │
│  Cost: $0 (CodeDeploy EC2 free) + $1 (CodeBuild) = $1/month                                      │
│  Developer Time Saved: 10 hours/month (vs manual deployment)                                     │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘


┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          🎨 DEPLOYMENT STRATEGIES COMPARISON (VISUAL)                              │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

BLUE/GREEN DEPLOYMENT (Zero Downtime):          IN-PLACE ROLLING DEPLOYMENT (Partial Availability):
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  BLUE (Current)     GREEN (New)     │         │  Rolling Update: OneAtATime         │
│  ┌──────────┐      ┌──────────┐    │         │  ┌──────────┐┌──────────┐┌──────────┐
│  │ v1.2.2   │      │ v1.2.3   │    │         │  │ v1.2.2   ││ v1.2.2   ││ v1.2.2   │
│  │ EC2-1    │      │ EC2-4    │    │         │  │ EC2-1    ││ EC2-2    ││ EC2-3    │
│  │ EC2-2    │      │ EC2-5    │    │         │  │ ✅ Active││ ✅ Active││ ✅ Active│
│  │ EC2-3    │      │ EC2-6    │    │         │  └──────────┘└──────────┘└──────────┘
│  │ ✅ Live  │      │ 🧪 Test  │    │         │       ↓           ↓           ↓      │
│  └──────────┘      └──────────┘    │         │  ┌──────────┐┌──────────┐┌──────────┐
│       ↓                  ↓          │         │  │ v1.2.3   ││ v1.2.2   ││ v1.2.2   │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │         │  │ EC2-1    ││ EC2-2    ││ EC2-3    │
│  │    ALB TRAFFIC SWITCH        │   │         │  │ ✅ Active││ ✅ Active││ ✅ Active│
│  │    100% Blue → 100% Green    │   │         │  └──────────┘└──────────┘└──────────┘
│  └─────────────────────────────┘   │         │       ↓           ↓           ↓      │
│  Keep Blue for quick rollback      │         │  Continue until all updated...       │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘         └─────────────────────────────────────┘
    ⏱️ Switch time: 30 seconds                      ⏱️ Total time: 15 minutes
