└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🧠 2. Core Concepts

📈 1. Metrics

Metrics are time-ordered data points representing the behavior of your resources. CloudWatch stores metrics for up to 15 months.

Metric Type Description anihpj Example Cost
Basic (5-min) Default metrics from AWS services EC2 CPUUtilization, NetworkIn/Out FREE
Detailed (1-min) Higher resolution for faster detection EC2 detailed monitoring $2.10/instance/mo
High-Res (1-sec) Real-time monitoring for custom metrics Django request latency per second $0.30/metric/mo
Custom Application-specific metrics Job posts created/hour, users online $0.30/metric/mo
Publishing Custom Metrics from Django (anihpj): 
import boto3
from datetime import datetime

cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch', region_name='us-east-1')

# Track job posts created
def track_job_post_created():
    cloudwatch.put_metric_data(
        Namespace='anihpj/JobPortal',
        MetricData=[{
            'MetricName': 'JobPostsCreated',
            'Value': 1,
            'Unit': 'Count',
            'Timestamp': datetime.utcnow(),
            'Dimensions': [
                {'Name': 'Environment', 'Value': 'production'},
                {'Name': 'Category', 'Value': 'TechJobs'}
            ]
        }]
    )

# Track user registrations
def track_user_signup(user_type):
    cloudwatch.put_metric_data(
        Namespace='anihpj/JobPortal',
        MetricData=[{
            'MetricName': 'UserSignups',
            'Value': 1,
            'Unit': 'Count',
            'Timestamp': datetime.utcnow(),
            'Dimensions': [
                {'Name': 'UserType', 'Value': user_type},  # 'JobSeeker' or 'Employer'
                {'Name': 'Platform', 'Value': 'Web'}
            ]
        }]
    )

# Track Django request latency
def track_request_latency(view_name, duration_ms):
    cloudwatch.put_metric_data(
        Namespace='anihpj/Django',
        MetricData=[{
            'MetricName': 'RequestLatency',
            'Value': duration_ms,
            'Unit': 'Milliseconds',
            'Timestamp': datetime.utcnow(),
            'Dimensions': [
                {'Name': 'ViewName', 'Value': view_name},
                {'Name': 'Server', 'Value': 'anihpj-web-1'}
            ]
        }]
    )
📝 2. Logs

CloudWatch Logs enables you to centralize logs from all your systems, applications, and AWS services in a single, highly scalable service.

Log Hierarchy Structure:
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 CLOUDWATCH LOGS STRUCTURE                  │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                            │
│  Log Groups (Containers for log streams)                  │
│  ├─ /aws/ec2/anihpj-web                                   │
│  │  └─ Log Streams (Individual sources)                   │
│  │     ├─ i-abc123 (Instance 1)                           │
│  │     ├─ i-def456 (Instance 2)                           │
│  │     └─ i-ghi789 (Instance 3)                           │
│  │                                                         │
│  ├─ /aws/lambda/send-job-alerts                           │
│  │  └─ Log Streams                                        │
│  │     ├─ 2026/01/08/[$LATEST]abc123                      │
│  │     └─ 2026/01/08/[$LATEST]def456                      │
│  │                                                         │
│  ├─ /anihpj/django/application                            │
│  │  └─ Log Streams                                        │
│  │     ├─ anihpj-web-1 (Django logs)                      │
│  │     └─ anihpj-web-2 (Django logs)                      │
│  │                                                         │
│  ├─ /anihpj/nginx/access                                  │
│  │  └─ Log Streams                                        │
│  │     ├─ access-2026-01-08                               │
│  │     └─ access-2026-01-07                               │
│  │                                                         │
│  └─ /aws/rds/instance/anihpj-db/postgresql                │
│     └─ Log Streams                                        │
│        └─ postgresql.log.2026-01-08                       │
│                                                            │
│  Log Events (Individual log entries with timestamp)       │
│  [2026-01-08T10:30:45] ERROR: Failed to send email...     │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Django Logging Configuration for CloudWatch: 
# anihpj/settings.py
import boto3
from watchtower import CloudWatchLogHandler

LOGGING = {
    'version': 1,
    'disable_existing_loggers': False,
    'formatters': {
        'verbose': {
            'format': '[{levelname}] {asctime} {module} {message}',
            'style': '{',
        },
    },
    'handlers': {
        'console': {
            'class': 'logging.StreamHandler',
            'formatter': 'verbose',
        },
        'cloudwatch': {
            'class': 'watchtower.CloudWatchLogHandler',
            'boto3_client': boto3.client('logs', region_name='us-east-1'),
            'log_group': '/anihpj/django/application',
            'stream_name': 'anihpj-web-1',
            'formatter': 'verbose',
        },
    },
    'loggers': {
        'django': {
            'handlers': ['console', 'cloudwatch'],
            'level': 'INFO',
        },
        'jobpost': {
            'handlers': ['console', 'cloudwatch'],
            'level': 'DEBUG',
        },
    },
}

# requirements.txt
# watchtower==3.0.1
# boto3==1.28.0
🚨 3. Alarms

CloudWatch Alarms watch a single metric and perform actions when the metric breaches a threshold.

Alarm State Description Action
✅ OK Metric is within threshold No action
🔴 ALARM Metric breached threshold Send SNS notification, scale resources
⚪ INSUFFICIENT_DATA Not enough data to determine state Wait for more data
Creating Alarms for anihpj (AWS CLI): 
# High CPU Alarm
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name anihpj-high-cpu \
    --alarm-description "Alert when EC2 CPU exceeds 80%" \
    --metric-name CPUUtilization \
    --namespace AWS/EC2 \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 300 \
    --evaluation-periods 2 \
    --threshold 80 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --dimensions Name=InstanceId,Value=i-abc123 \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-alerts

# High Memory Alarm
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name anihpj-high-memory \
    --alarm-description "Alert when memory exceeds 90%" \
    --metric-name mem_used_percent \
    --namespace CWAgent \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 300 \
    --evaluation-periods 1 \
    --threshold 90 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --dimensions Name=InstanceId,Value=i-abc123 \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-alerts

# Django Error Rate Alarm
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name anihpj-error-rate-high \
    --alarm-description "Alert when Django errors exceed 10/min" \
    --metric-name ErrorCount \
    --namespace anihpj/Django \
    --statistic Sum \
    --period 60 \
    --evaluation-periods 1 \
    --threshold 10 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-critical
📊 4. Dashboards

CloudWatch Dashboards are customizable home pages where you can monitor resources in a single view.

✅ Free Tier: First 3 dashboards (up to 50 metrics each) are FREE!

🚀 3. Getting Started with CloudWatch for Django

🎯 What We'll Build:
  • CloudWatch agent on EC2 for system metrics
  • Django application logging to CloudWatch Logs
  • Custom metrics for job portal (posts/hour, user signups)
  • Alarms for CPU, memory, errors, and latency
  • Dashboard visualizing anihpj health
⏱️ Total Setup Time: 45 minutes
📋 Step 1: Prerequisites
  • ✅ AWS account with CloudWatch permissions
  • ✅ EC2 instance running anihpj Django application
  • ✅ IAM role with CloudWatchAgentServerPolicy attached to EC2
  • ✅ SNS topic for alarm notifications
  • ✅ boto3 and watchtower installed in Django environment
⚙️ Step 2: Install CloudWatch Agent on EC2
#!/bin/bash
# Install CloudWatch agent on Ubuntu 20.04/22.04

# Download agent
wget https://s3.amazonaws.com/amazoncloudwatch-agent/ubuntu/amd64/latest/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.deb

# Install
sudo dpkg -i amazon-cloudwatch-agent.deb

# Create configuration file
sudo cat > /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/etc/config.json <<'EOF'
{
  "agent": {
    "metrics_collection_interval": 60,
    "run_as_user": "cwagent"
  },
  "metrics": {
    "namespace": "anihpj/EC2",
    "metrics_collected": {
      "cpu": {
        "measurement": [
          {"name": "cpu_usage_idle", "rename": "CPU_IDLE", "unit": "Percent"},
          {"name": "cpu_usage_iowait", "rename": "CPU_IOWAIT", "unit": "Percent"},
          "cpu_time_guest"
        ],
        "metrics_collection_interval": 60,
        "totalcpu": false
      },
      "disk": {
        "measurement": [
          {"name": "used_percent", "rename": "DISK_USED", "unit": "Percent"},
          "inodes_free"
        ],
        "metrics_collection_interval": 60,
        "resources": [
          "*"
        ]
      },
      "mem": {
        "measurement": [
          {"name": "mem_used_percent", "rename": "MEM_USED", "unit": "Percent"}
        ],
        "metrics_collection_interval": 60
      },
      "netstat": {
        "measurement": [
          "tcp_established",
          "tcp_time_wait"
        ],
        "metrics_collection_interval": 60
      }
    }
  },
  "logs": {
    "logs_collected": {
      "files": {
        "collect_list": [
          {
            "file_path": "/var/log/syslog",
            "log_group_name": "/anihpj/system/syslog",
            "log_stream_name": "{instance_id}"
          },
          {
            "file_path": "/var/log/nginx/access.log",
            "log_group_name": "/anihpj/nginx/access",
            "log_stream_name": "{instance_id}"
          },
          {
            "file_path": "/var/log/nginx/error.log",
            "log_group_name": "/anihpj/nginx/error",
            "log_stream_name": "{instance_id}"
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
EOF

# Start agent
sudo /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl \
    -a fetch-config \
    -m ec2 \
    -s \
    -c file:/opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/etc/config.json

# Verify agent is running
sudo /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl \
    -m ec2 \
    -a query

# Enable agent on boot
sudo systemctl enable amazon-cloudwatch-agent
🐍 Step 3: Configure Django Logging
# Install dependencies
pip install watchtower boto3

# Add to requirements.txt
echo "watchtower==3.0.1" >> requirements.txt
echo "boto3==1.28.0" >> requirements.txt
# anihpj/settings.py
import boto3
from watchtower import CloudWatchLogHandler

# CloudWatch configuration
CLOUDWATCH_LOG_GROUP = '/anihpj/django/application'
CLOUDWATCH_STREAM_NAME = f'anihpj-web-{os.environ.get("INSTANCE_ID", "local")}'

LOGGING = {
    'version': 1,
    'disable_existing_loggers': False,
    'formatters': {
        'verbose': {
            'format': '[{levelname}] {asctime} {module} {process:d} {thread:d} {message}',
            'style': '{',
        },
        'simple': {
            'format': '{levelname} {message}',
            'style': '{',
        },
    },
    'handlers': {
        'console': {
            'level': 'INFO',
            'class': 'logging.StreamHandler',
            'formatter': 'verbose'
        },
        'cloudwatch': {
            'level': 'INFO',
            'class': 'watchtower.CloudWatchLogHandler',
            'boto3_client': boto3.client('logs', region_name='us-east-1'),
            'log_group': CLOUDWATCH_LOG_GROUP,
            'stream_name': CLOUDWATCH_STREAM_NAME,
            'formatter': 'verbose',
        },
        'file': {
            'level': 'INFO',
            'class': 'logging.handlers.RotatingFileHandler',
            'filename': '/var/log/anihpj/django.log',
            'maxBytes': 1024 * 1024 * 5,  # 5 MB
            'backupCount': 5,
            'formatter': 'verbose',
        },
    },
    'loggers': {
        'django': {
            'handlers': ['console', 'cloudwatch', 'file'],
            'level': 'INFO',
            'propagate': False,
        },
        'jobpost': {
            'handlers': ['console', 'cloudwatch', 'file'],
            'level': 'DEBUG',
            'propagate': False,
        },
        'anihpj': {
            'handlers': ['console', 'cloudwatch', 'file'],
            'level': 'DEBUG',
            'propagate': False,
        },
    },
    'root': {
        'handlers': ['console'],
        'level': 'WARNING',
    },
}
📊 Step 4: Create anihpj Dashboard
# Create dashboard via AWS CLI
aws cloudwatch put-dashboard \
    --dashboard-name anihpj-production \
    --dashboard-body file://anihpj-dashboard.json
// anihpj-dashboard.json
{
  "widgets": [
    {
      "type": "metric",
      "properties": {
        "metrics": [
          [ "AWS/EC2", "CPUUtilization", { "stat": "Average" } ],
          [ ".", "NetworkIn", { "stat": "Sum" } ],
          [ ".", "NetworkOut", { "stat": "Sum" } ]
        ],
        "period": 300,
        "stat": "Average",
        "region": "us-east-1",
        "title": "EC2 Performance",
        "yAxis": {
          "left": {
            "min": 0,
            "max": 100
          }
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "metric",
      "properties": {
        "metrics": [
          [ "anihpj/JobPortal", "JobPostsCreated", { "stat": "Sum" } ],
          [ ".", "UserSignups", { "stat": "Sum" } ]
        ],
        "period": 3600,
        "stat": "Sum",
        "region": "us-east-1",
        "title": "User Activity"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "log",
      "properties": {
        "query": "SOURCE '/anihpj/django/application' | fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /ERROR/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 20",
        "region": "us-east-1",
        "title": "Recent Errors"
      }
    }
  ]
}
🚨 Step 5: Create Critical Alarms
#!/bin/bash
# Create SNS topic for alerts
TOPIC_ARN=$(aws sns create-topic \
    --name anihpj-cloudwatch-alerts \
    --query 'TopicArn' \
    --output text)

# Subscribe email to topic
aws sns subscribe \
    --topic-arn $TOPIC_ARN \
    --protocol email \
    --notification-endpoint team@anihpj.com

# High CPU Alarm
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name "anihpj-prod-high-cpu" \
    --alarm-description "CPU exceeds 80% for 10 minutes" \
    --metric-name CPUUtilization \
    --namespace AWS/EC2 \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 300 \
    --evaluation-periods 2 \
    --threshold 80 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --dimensions Name=InstanceId,Value=i-abc123 \
    --alarm-actions $TOPIC_ARN

# Low Disk Space Alarm
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name "anihpj-prod-low-disk" \
    --alarm-description "Disk usage exceeds 85%" \
    --metric-name DISK_USED \
    --namespace anihpj/EC2 \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 300 \
    --evaluation-periods 1 \
    --threshold 85 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --alarm-actions $TOPIC_ARN

# Django Error Rate Alarm
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name "anihpj-prod-error-spike" \
    --alarm-description "More than 10 errors per minute" \
    --metric-name ErrorCount \
    --namespace anihpj/Django \
    --statistic Sum \
    --period 60 \
    --evaluation-periods 1 \
    --threshold 10 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --alarm-actions $TOPIC_ARN

echo "✅ Alarms created successfully!"
echo "📧 Confirm email subscription to receive alerts"
🎉 Setup Complete!

Your anihpj monitoring is now active:

  • ✅ CloudWatch agent collecting system metrics (CPU, memory, disk)
  • ✅ Django logs streaming to CloudWatch Logs
  • ✅ Dashboard visualizing key metrics
  • ✅ Alarms configured for critical thresholds
  • ✅ Email notifications enabled via SNS

Next: Visit CloudWatch console → Dashboards → anihpj-production to see your metrics!
